# Funcy

A collection of fancy functional tools focused on practicality.

Inspired by clojure, underscore and my own abstractions. Keep reading to get an overview
or [read the docs](http://funcy.readthedocs.org/).
Or jump directly to [cheatsheet](http://funcy.readthedocs.io/en/stable/cheatsheet.html).

Works with Python 3.4+ and pypy3.

## Installation

```
pip install funcy
```

## Overview

Import stuff from funcy to make things happen:

```python
from funcy import whatever, you, need
```

Merge collections of same type
(works for dicts, sets, lists, tuples, iterators and even strings):

```python
merge(coll1, coll2, coll3, ...)
join(colls)
merge_with(sum, dict1, dict2, ...)
```

Walk through collection, creating its transform (like map but preserves type):

```python
walk(str.upper, {'a', 'b'})            # {'A', 'B'}
walk(reversed, {'a': 1, 'b': 2})       # {1: 'a', 2: 'b'}
walk_keys(double, {'a': 1, 'b': 2})    # {'aa': 1, 'bb': 2}
walk_values(inc, {'a': 1, 'b': 2})     # {'a': 2, 'b': 3}
```

Select a part of collection:

```python
select(even, {1,2,3,10,20})                  # {2,10,20}
select(r'^a', ('a','b','ab','ba'))           # ('a','ab')
select_keys(callable, {str: '', None: None}) # {str: ''}
compact({2, None, 1, 0})                     # {1,2}
```

Manipulate sequences:

```python
take(4, iterate(double, 1)) # [1, 2, 4, 8]
first(drop(3, count(10)))   # 13

lremove(even, [1, 2, 3])    # [1, 3]
lconcat([1, 2], [5, 6])     # [1, 2, 5, 6]
lcat(map(range, range(4)))  # [0, 0, 1, 0, 1, 2]
lmapcat(range, range(4))    # same
flatten(nested_structure)   # flat iter
distinct('abacbdd')         # iter('abcd')

lsplit(odd, range(5))       # ([1, 3], [0, 2, 4])
lsplit_at(2, range(5))      # ([0, 1], [2, 3, 4])
group_by(mod3, range(5))    # {0: [0, 3], 1: [1, 4], 2: [2]}

lpartition(2, range(5))     # [[0, 1], [2, 3]]
chunks(2, range(5))         # iter: [0, 1], [2, 3], [4]
pairwise(range(5))          # iter: [0, 1], [1, 2], ...
```

And functions:

```python
partial(add, 1)             # inc
curry(add)(1)(2)            # 3
compose(inc, double)(10)    # 21
complement(even)            # odd
all_fn(isa(int), even)      # is_even_int

one_third = rpartial(operator.div, 3.0)
has_suffix = rcurry(str.endswith, 2)
```

Create decorators easily:

```python
@decorator
def log(call):
    print call._func.__name__, call._args
    return call()
```

Abstract control flow:

```python
walk_values(silent(int), {'a': '1', 'b': 'no'})
# => {'a': 1, 'b': None}

@once
def initialize():
    "..."

with suppress(OSError):
    os.remove('some.file')

@ignore(ErrorRateExceeded)
@limit_error_rate(fails=5, timeout=60)
@retry(tries=2, errors=(HttpError, ServiceDown))
def some_unreliable_action(...):
    "..."

class MyUser(AbstractBaseUser):
    @cached_property
    def public_phones(self):
        return self.phones.filter(public=True)
```

Ease debugging:

```python
squares = {tap(x, 'x'): tap(x * x, 'x^2') for x in [3, 4]}
# x: 3
# x^2: 9
# ...

@print_exits
def some_func(...):
    "..."

@log_calls(log.info, errors=False)
@log_errors(log.exception)
def some_suspicious_function(...):
    "..."

with print_durations('Creating models'):
    Model.objects.create(...)
    # ...
# 10.2 ms in Creating models
```

And [much more](http://funcy.readthedocs.org/).

## Dive in

Funcy is an embodiment of ideas I explain in several essays:

- [Why Every Language Needs Its Underscore](https://suor.github.io/blog/2014/06/22/why-every-language-needs-its-underscore/)
- [Functional Python Made Easy](https://suor.github.io/blog/2013/10/13/functional-python-made-easy/)
- [Abstracting Control Flow](https://suor.github.io/blog/2013/10/08/abstracting-control-flow/)
- [Painless Decorators](https://suor.github.io/blog/2013/11/03/painless-decorators/)

## Running tests

To run the tests using your default python:

```
pip install -r test_requirements.txt
py.test
```

To fully run `tox` you need all the supported pythons to be installed. These are
3.4+ and PyPy3. You can run it for particular environment even in absense
of all of the above:

```
tox -e py310
tox -e pypy3
tox -e lint
```

variables.name: funcy.compat.basestring
documentation: noqa

functions.name: funcy.funcolls.all_fn
documentation: Constructs a predicate, which holds when all fs hold.

functions.name: funcy.funcolls.any_fn
documentation: Constructs a predicate, which holds when any fs holds.

functions.name: funcy.funcolls.none_fn
documentation: Constructs a predicate, which holds when none of fs hold.

functions.name: funcy.funcolls.one_fn
documentation: Constructs a predicate, which holds when exactly one of fs holds.

functions.name: funcy.funcolls.some_fn
documentation:
Constructs a function, which calls fs one by one
and returns first truthy result.

functions.name: funcy.calc.memoize
documentation:
@memoize(key_func=None). Makes decorated function memoize its results.

If key_func is specified uses key_func(*func_args, **func_kwargs) as memory key.
Otherwise uses args + tuple(sorted(kwargs.items()))

Exposes its memory via .memory attribute.
TODO: use pos-only arg once in Python 3.8+ only

functions.name: funcy.calc.cache
documentation: Caches a function results for timeout seconds.

functions.name: funcy.py3.lzip
documentation: List zip() version.

variables.name: funcy.primitives.EMPTY
documentation: Used as unique default for optional arguments

functions.name: funcy.types.isa
documentation:
Creates a function checking if its argument
is of any of given types.

modules.name: funcy.py2
documentation:
Rewrite function names to represent Python 2 list-by-default interface.
Iterator versions go with i prefix.

functions.name: funcy.tree.tree_leaves
documentation: Iterates over tree leaves.

functions.name: funcy.tree.ltree_leaves
documentation: Lists tree leaves.

functions.name: funcy.tree.tree_nodes
documentation: Iterates over all tree nodes.

functions.name: funcy.tree.ltree_nodes
documentation: Lists all tree nodes.

functions.name: funcy.strings.re_iter
documentation: Iterates over matches of regex in s, presents them in simplest possible form

functions.name: funcy.strings.re_all
documentation: Lists all matches of regex in s, presents them in simplest possible form

functions.name: funcy.strings.re_find
documentation:
Matches regex against the given string,
returns the match in the simplest possible form.

functions.name: funcy.strings.re_test
documentation: Tests whether regex matches against s.

functions.name: funcy.strings.re_finder
documentation: Creates a function finding regex in passed string.

functions.name: funcy.strings.re_tester
documentation: Creates a predicate testing passed string with regex.

functions.name: funcy.strings.str_join
documentation:
Joins the given sequence with sep.
Forces stringification of seq items.

functions.name: funcy.strings.cut_prefix
documentation: Cuts prefix from given string if it's present.

functions.name: funcy.strings.cut_suffix
documentation: Cuts suffix from given string if it's present.

variables.name: funcy.strings._re_type
documentation: re.Pattern was added in Python 3.7

functions.name: funcy.funcs.identity
documentation: Returns its argument.

functions.name: funcy.funcs.constantly
documentation: Creates a function accepting any args, but always returning x.

functions.name: funcy.funcs.caller
documentation: Creates a function calling its sole argument with given *a, **kw.
an operator.methodcaller() brother

functions.name: funcy.funcs.func_partial
documentation:
A functools.partial alternative, which returns a real function.
Can be used to construct methods.

functions.name: funcy.funcs.rpartial
documentation:
Partially applies last arguments.
New keyworded arguments extend and override kwargs.

functions.name: funcy.funcs.curry
documentation: Curries func into a chain of one argument functions.

functions.name: funcy.funcs.rcurry
documentation:
Curries func into a chain of one argument functions.
Arguments are passed from right to left.

functions.name: funcy.funcs.autocurry
documentation:
Creates a version of func returning its partial applications
until sufficient arguments are passed.

functions.name: funcy.funcs.iffy
documentation: Creates a function, which conditionally applies action or default.

functions.name: funcy.funcs.compose
documentation: Composes passed functions.

functions.name: funcy.funcs.rcompose
documentation: Composes functions, calling them from left to right.

functions.name: funcy.funcs.complement
documentation: Constructs a complementary predicate.

functions.name: funcy.funcs.ljuxt
documentation:
Constructs a juxtaposition of the given functions.
Result returns a list of results of fs.
NOTE: using lazy map in these two will result in empty list/iterator
from all calls to i?juxt result since map iterator will be depleted

functions.name: funcy.funcs.juxt
documentation:
Constructs a lazy juxtaposition of the given functions.
Result returns an iterator of results of fs.

functions.name: funcy.objects.wrap_prop
documentation: Wrap a property accessors with a context manager

functions.name: funcy.objects.monkey
documentation:
Monkey patches class or module by adding to it decorated function.

Anything overwritten could be accessed via .original attribute of decorated object.

classes.name: funcy.objects.cached_property
documentation:
Decorator that converts a method with a single self argument into
a property cached on the instance.

classes.name: funcy.objects.cached_readonly
documentation: Same as @cached_property, but protected against rewrites.

classes.name: funcy.objects.LazyObject

methods.name: funcy.objects.LazyObject.__init__
documentation: NOTE: we can add lots of magic methods here to intercept on more events,
this is postponed. As well as metaclass to support isinstance() check.

A simplistic lazy init object.
Rewrites itself when any attribute is accessed.
TODO: monkey_mix()?

functions.name: funcy.debug.tap
documentation: Prints x and then returns it.

functions.name: funcy.debug.log_calls
documentation:
Logs or prints all function calls,
including arguments, results and raised exceptions.

functions.name: funcy.debug.log_enters
documentation: Logs each entrance to a function.

functions.name: funcy.debug.print_enters
documentation: Prints on each entrance to a function.

functions.name: funcy.debug.log_exits
documentation: Logs exits from a function.

functions.name: funcy.debug.print_exits
documentation: Prints on exits from a function.

functions.name: funcy.debug.format_time
documentation: Duration utils

functions.name: funcy.debug.log_iter_durations
documentation: Times processing of each item in seq.

functions.name: funcy.debug.print_iter_durations
documentation: Times processing of each item in seq.

functions.name: funcy.debug._format_error
documentation: ## Formatting utils

functions.name: funcy.debug.signature_repr
documentation: ## Call signature stringification utils

classes.name: funcy.debug.LabeledContextDecorator
documentation: A context manager which also works as decorator, passing call signature as its label.

classes.name: funcy.debug.log_errors
documentation: Logs or prints all errors within a function or block.

classes.name: funcy.debug.log_durations
documentation: Times each function call or block execution.

functions.name: funcy.flow.raiser
documentation:
Constructs function that raises the given exception
with given arguments on any invocation.
## Error handling utilities

functions.name: funcy.flow.ignore
documentation: Alters function to ignore given errors, returning default instead.
Not using @decorator here for speed,
since @ignore and @silent should be used for very simple and fast functions

functions.name: funcy.flow.silent
documentation: Alters function to ignore all exceptions.

functions.name: funcy.flow.reraise
documentation: Reraises errors as other exception.

functions.name: funcy.flow.retry
documentation:
Makes decorated function retry up to tries times.
Retries only on specified errors.
Sleeps timeout or timeout(attempt) seconds between tries.

functions.name: funcy.flow.fallback
documentation:
Tries several approaches until one works.
Each approach has a form of (callable, expected_errors).

functions.name: funcy.flow._ensure_exceptable
documentation:
Ensures that errors are passable to except clause.
I.e. should be BaseException subclass or a tuple.

functions.name: funcy.flow.limit_error_rate
documentation:
If function fails to complete fails times in a row,
calls to it will be intercepted for timeout with exception raised instead.

functions.name: funcy.flow.throttle
documentation: Allows only one run in a period, the rest is skipped

functions.name: funcy.flow.post_processing
documentation: Post processes decorated function result with func.
## Post processing decorators

functions.name: funcy.flow.joining
documentation: Joins decorated function results with sep.

functions.name: funcy.flow.once_per
documentation: Call function only once for every combination of the given arguments.
## Initialization helpers

functions.name: funcy.flow.once_per_args
documentation: Call function once for every combination of values of its arguments.

functions.name: funcy.flow.wrap_with
documentation: Turn context manager into a decorator

classes.name: funcy.flow.nullcontext
documentation:
Context manager that does no additional processing.

Used as a stand-in for a normal context manager, when a particular
block of code is only sometimes used with a normal context manager:

cm = optional_cm if condition else nullcontext()
with cm:
    # Perform operation, using optional_cm if condition is True

functions.name: funcy.decorators.decorator
documentation:
Transforms a flat wrapper into decorator:

```
@decorator
def func(call, methods, content_type=DEFAULT):  # These are decorator params
    # Access call arg by name
    if call.request.method not in methods:
        # ...
    # Decorated functions and all the arguments are accesible as:
    print(call._func, call_args, call._kwargs)
    # Finally make a call:
    return call()
```

functions.name: funcy.decorators.update_wrapper
documentation: ## Add __original__ to update_wrapper and @wraps

classes.name: funcy.decorators.Call
documentation:
A call object to pass as first argument to decorator.

Call object is just a proxy for decorated function
with call arguments saved in its attributes.

functions.name: funcy.colls.empty
documentation: Creates an empty collection of the same type.

functions.name: funcy.colls.join
documentation: Joins several collections of same type into one.

functions.name: funcy.colls.merge
documentation:
Merges several collections of same type into one.

Works with dicts, sets, lists, tuples, iterators and strings.
For dicts later values take precedence.

functions.name: funcy.colls.join_with
documentation: Joins several dicts, combining values with given function.

functions.name: funcy.colls.merge_with
documentation: Merges several dicts, combining values with given function.

functions.name: funcy.colls.walk
documentation:
Walks the collection transforming its elements with f.
Same as map, but preserves coll type.

functions.name: funcy.colls.walk_keys
documentation: Walks keys of the collection, mapping them with f.

functions.name: funcy.colls.walk_values
documentation: Walks values of the collection, mapping them with f.

functions.name: funcy.colls.select
documentation: Same as filter but preserves coll type.
TODO: prewalk, postwalk and friends

functions.name: funcy.colls.select_keys
documentation: Select part of the collection with keys passing pred.

functions.name: funcy.colls.select_values
documentation: Select part of the collection with values passing pred.

functions.name: funcy.colls.compact
documentation: Removes falsy values from the collection.

functions.name: funcy.colls.is_distinct
documentation: Checks if all elements in the collection are different.
## Content tests

functions.name: funcy.colls.all
documentation: Checks if all items in seq pass pred (or are truthy).

functions.name: funcy.colls.any
documentation: Checks if any item in seq passes pred (or is truthy).

functions.name: funcy.colls.none
documentation: "Checks if none of the items in seq pass pred (or are truthy).

functions.name: funcy.colls.one
documentation: Checks whether exactly one item in seq passes pred (or is truthy).

functions.name: funcy.colls.some
documentation: Finds first item in seq passing pred or first that is truthy.
Not same as in clojure! returns value found not pred(value)

functions.name: funcy.colls.zipdict
documentation: Creates a dict with keys mapped to the corresponding vals.
TODO: a variant of some that returns mapped value,
one can use some(map(f, seq)) or first(keep(f, seq)) for now.
TODO: vector comparison tests - ascending, descending and such
def chain_test(compare, seq):
return all(compare, zip(seq, rest(seq))

functions.name: funcy.colls.flip
documentation: Flip passed dict or collection of pairs swapping its keys and values.

functions.name: funcy.colls.project
documentation: Leaves only given keys in mapping.

functions.name: funcy.colls.omit
documentation: Removes given keys from mapping.

functions.name: funcy.colls.zip_values
documentation: Yields tuples of corresponding values of several dicts.

functions.name: funcy.colls.zip_dicts
documentation:
Yields tuples like (key, (val1, val2, ...))
for each common key in all given dicts.

functions.name: funcy.colls.get_in
documentation: Returns a value at path in the given nested collection.

functions.name: funcy.colls.get_lax
documentation:
Returns a value at path in the given nested collection.
Does not raise on a wrong collection type along the way, but removes default.

functions.name: funcy.colls.set_in
documentation: Creates a copy of coll with the value set at path.

functions.name: funcy.colls.update_in
documentation: Creates a copy of coll with a value updated at path.

functions.name: funcy.colls.del_in
documentation: Creates a copy of coll with a nested key or index deleted.

functions.name: funcy.colls.has_path
documentation: Checks if path exists in the given nested collection.

functions.name: funcy.colls.lwhere
documentation: Selects mappings containing all pairs in cond.

functions.name: funcy.colls.lpluck
documentation: Lists values for key in each mapping.

functions.name: funcy.colls.lpluck_attr
documentation: Lists values of given attribute of each object.

functions.name: funcy.colls.linvoke
documentation:
Makes a list of results of the obj.name(*args, **kwargs)
for each object in objects.

functions.name: funcy.colls.where
documentation: Iterates over mappings containing all pairs in cond.
Iterator versions for python 3 interface

functions.name: funcy.colls.pluck
documentation: Iterates over values for key in mappings.

functions.name: funcy.colls.pluck_attr
documentation: Iterates over values of given attribute of given objects.

functions.name: funcy.colls.invoke
documentation:
Yields results of the obj.name(*args, **kwargs)
for each object in objects.

functions.name: funcy.seqs.repeatedly
documentation:
Takes a function of no args, presumably with side effects,
and returns an infinite (or length n) iterator of calls to it.

functions.name: funcy.seqs.iterate
documentation: Returns an infinite iterator of `x, f(x), f(f(x)), ...`

functions.name: funcy.seqs.take
documentation:
Returns a list of first n items in the sequence,
or all items if there are fewer than n.

functions.name: funcy.seqs.drop
documentation: Skips first n items in the sequence, yields the rest.

functions.name: funcy.seqs.first
documentation:
Returns the first item in the sequence.
Returns None if the sequence is empty.

functions.name: funcy.seqs.second
documentation:
Returns second item in the sequence.
Returns None if there are less than two items in it.

functions.name: funcy.seqs.nth
documentation: Returns nth item in the sequence or None if no such item exists.

functions.name: funcy.seqs.last
documentation:
Returns the last item in the sequence or iterator.
Returns None if the sequence is empty.

functions.name: funcy.seqs.rest
documentation: Skips first item in the sequence, yields the rest.

functions.name: funcy.seqs.butlast
documentation: Iterates over all elements of the sequence but last.

functions.name: funcy.seqs.ilen
documentation:
Consumes an iterable not reading it into memory
and returns the number of items.

functions.name: funcy.seqs.lmap
documentation:
An extended version of builtin map() returning a list.
Derives a mapper from string, int, slice, dict or set.
TODO: tree-seq equivalent

functions.name: funcy.seqs.lfilter
documentation:
An extended version of builtin filter() returning a list.
Derives a predicate from string, int, slice, dict or set.

functions.name: funcy.seqs.map
documentation:
An extended version of builtin map().
Derives a mapper from string, int, slice, dict or set.

functions.name: funcy.seqs.filter
documentation:
An extended version of builtin filter().
Derives a predicate from string, int, slice, dict or set.

functions.name: funcy.seqs.lremove
documentation: Creates a list if items passing given predicate.

functions.name: funcy.seqs.remove
documentation: Iterates items passing given predicate.

functions.name: funcy.seqs.lkeep
documentation:
Maps seq with f and keeps only truthy results.
Simply lists truthy values in one argument version.

functions.name: funcy.seqs.keep
documentation:
Maps seq with f and iterates truthy results.
Simply iterates truthy values in one argument version.

functions.name: funcy.seqs.without
documentation: Iterates over sequence skipping items.

functions.name: funcy.seqs.lwithout
documentation: Removes items from sequence, preserves order.

functions.name: funcy.seqs.lconcat
documentation: Concatenates several sequences.

functions.name: funcy.seqs.lcat
documentation: Concatenates the sequence of sequences.

functions.name: funcy.seqs.flatten
documentation:
Flattens arbitrary nested sequence.
Unpacks an item if follow(item) is truthy.

functions.name: funcy.seqs.lflatten
documentation:
Iterates over arbitrary nested sequence.
Dives into when follow(item) is truthy.

functions.name: funcy.seqs.lmapcat
documentation: Maps given sequence(s) and concatenates the results.

functions.name: funcy.seqs.mapcat
documentation: Maps given sequence(s) and chains the results.

functions.name: funcy.seqs.interleave
documentation: Yields first item of each sequence, then second one and so on.

functions.name: funcy.seqs.interpose
documentation: Yields items of the sequence alternating with sep.

functions.name: funcy.seqs.takewhile
documentation:
Yields sequence items until first predicate fail.
Stops on first falsy value in one argument version.

functions.name: funcy.seqs.dropwhile
documentation:
Skips the start of the sequence passing pred (or just truthy),
then iterates over the rest.

functions.name: funcy.seqs.ldistinct
documentation: Removes duplicates from sequences, preserves order.

functions.name: funcy.seqs.distinct
documentation: Iterates over sequence skipping duplicates

functions.name: funcy.seqs.split
documentation:
Lazily splits items which pass the predicate from the ones that don't.
Returns a pair (passed, failed) of respective iterators.

functions.name: funcy.seqs.lsplit
documentation:
Splits items which pass the predicate from the ones that don't.
Returns a pair (passed, failed) of respective lists.

functions.name: funcy.seqs.split_at
documentation:
Lazily splits the sequence at given position,
returning a pair of iterators over its start and tail.

functions.name: funcy.seqs.lsplit_at
documentation:
Splits the sequence at given position,
returning a tuple of its start and tail.

functions.name: funcy.seqs.split_by
documentation:
Lazily splits the start of the sequence,
consisting of items passing pred, from the rest of it.

functions.name: funcy.seqs.lsplit_by
documentation:
Splits the start of the sequence,
consisting of items passing pred, from the rest of it.

functions.name: funcy.seqs.group_by
documentation: Groups given sequence items into a mapping f(item) -> [item, ...].

functions.name: funcy.seqs.group_by_keys
documentation:
Groups items having multiple keys into a mapping key -> [item, ...].
Item might be repeated under several keys.

functions.name: funcy.seqs.group_values
documentation: Takes a sequence of (key, value) pairs and groups values by keys.

functions.name: funcy.seqs.count_by
documentation:
Counts numbers of occurrences of values of f()
on elements of given sequence.

functions.name: funcy.seqs.count_reps
documentation: Counts number occurrences of each value in the sequence.

functions.name: funcy.seqs._cut_seq
documentation: For efficiency we use separate implementation for cutting sequences (those capable of slicing)

functions.name: funcy.seqs.partition
documentation:
Lazily partitions seq into parts of length n.
Skips step items between parts if passed. Non-fitting tail is ignored.

functions.name: funcy.seqs.lpartition
documentation:
Partitions seq into parts of length n.
Skips step items between parts if passed. Non-fitting tail is ignored.

functions.name: funcy.seqs.chunks
documentation:
Lazily chunks seq into parts of length n or less.
Skips step items between parts if passed.

functions.name: funcy.seqs.lchunks
documentation:
Chunks seq into parts of length n or less.
Skips step items between parts if passed.

functions.name: funcy.seqs.partition_by
documentation: Lazily partition seq into continuous chunks with constant value of f.

functions.name: funcy.seqs.lpartition_by
documentation: Partition seq into continuous chunks with constant value of f.

functions.name: funcy.seqs.with_prev
documentation: Yields each item paired with its preceding: (item, prev).

functions.name: funcy.seqs.with_next
documentation: Yields each item paired with its following: (item, next).

functions.name: funcy.seqs.pairwise
documentation: Yields all pairs of neighboring items in seq.
An itertools recipe
NOTE: this is the same as ipartition(2, 1, seq) only faster and with distinct name

functions.name: funcy.seqs.lzip
documentation: List zip() version.

functions.name: funcy.seqs.lzip
documentation: List zip() version.

functions.name: funcy.seqs.reductions
documentation: Yields intermediate reductions of seq by f.

functions.name: funcy.seqs.lreductions
